ddls:
CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "state_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each state */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region to which the state belongs */,
  "population" TEXT /* Total population of the state */,
  "female_population" TEXT /* Total female population of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "causes_of_death" (
  "cause_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cause of death */,
  "cause_name" TEXT /* Name of the cause of death */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the cause of death */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description or notes about the cause of death */,
  "is_preventable" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the cause of death is preventable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cause_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "deaths" (
  "death_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each death record */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* ID of the state where the death occurred */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of death */,
  "cause_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cause of death */,
  "upto14" INTEGER /* Number of deaths for age group upto 14 */,
  "15_29" INTEGER /* Number of deaths for age group 15-29 */,
  "30_44" INTEGER /* Number of deaths for age group 30-44 */,
  "45_59" INTEGER /* Number of deaths for age group 45-59 */,
  "60" INTEGER /* Number of deaths for age group 60+ */,
  "total_deaths" INTEGER /* Total number of deaths */,
  "male_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of male deaths */,
  "female_deaths" INTEGER /* Number of female deaths */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the occupation */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability of the data */,
  "data_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last data update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("death_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_deaths_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_deaths_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_deaths_cause_id FOREIGN KEY ("cause_id") REFERENCES causes_of_death ("cause_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation_name" TEXT /* Name of the occupation */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the occupation */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description or notes about the occupation */,
  "risk_level" TEXT /* Risk level associated with the occupation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)
